Hi Steven, good afternoon.

Need your help to check the official SonarQube PR validation process for the INT Services repo.

This is not related to my local SonarQube setup or local installation. This is about the actual Westpac project PR flow where the PR triggers the SonarQube inspection/check in Azure DevOps. I understand this pipeline/process was originally set up by you, so I wanted to refer back to you for the correct pattern.

The original Sonar code smells for SPESTG-480 were already fixed and pushed. The latest Sonar dashboard now shows 0 new issues, but the quality gate still fails because coverage on new code is 0%.

From the pipeline log, Sonar is looking for coverage files but none are generated/imported:

No test results files found
Did not find any coverage files in the expected location
Could not find any coverage report file matching coverage.opencover.xml or TestResults/coverage.xml

I tried adding a DotNetCoreCLI test step with coverlet/opencover output before Publish Test Results, since the existing SonarQubePrepare already has these properties:

sonar.cs.vscoveragexml.reportsPaths=$(coverageXml)
sonar.cs.opencover.reportsPaths=$(Agent.TempDirectory)/coverage.opencover.xml

But after rerun, the pipeline failed earlier at this existing NuGet install step:

Install Microsoft.PowerPlatform.Dataverse.Client to agent temp

Error shown:
Info_NuGetSupportedAfter3_5
Can't find loc string for key Info_NuGetSupportedAfter3_5

Can you help confirm the correct official pipeline pattern for generating/importing .NET coverage into SonarQube for this repo’s PR validation? I want to align with the existing Westpac project process instead of applying local or ad hoc changes.
